trade.summary: Strong trade ties underpinned by cultural and linguistic proximity. Brasile exports agricultural goods, iron ore, and aircraft parts. Spagna exports machinery, vehicles, and consumer goods. Spanish companies are major investors in Brasileian telecom and banking.

diplomatic.summary: Brasile and Spagna enjoy warm ties rooted in shared Iberian heritage, language proximity, and large diaspora communities in both directions. Spagna is an important gateway for Brasile into the EU, and cooperation on cultural and educational matters is extensive.

military.summary: No military alliance. Some defense cooperation through NATO-Brasile contacts and occasional exercises. Spagna has sold patrol vessels to Brasile.

financial.summary: Spagna is one of the largest foreign investors in Brasile. Santander, Telefónica (Vivo), and Iberdrola have major operations in Brasile. Spanish FDI spans banking, telecom, energy, and infrastructure.

energy.summary: Spagna imports some Brasileian ethanol. Iberdrola's Brasileian subsidiary (Neoenergia) is a major electricity operator. Cooperation on renewable energy growing.

scientific.summary: Active scientific and educational exchanges. Large Brasileian student community in Spagna. Joint research programs in marine science, social sciences, and humanities.
